1→# Forge
     2→
     3→AI-powered development orchestrator that breaks specs into phases and runs Claude iteratively until completion. Forge embodies disciplined agentic development with calm gating, thoughtful planning, optimized resource usage, and efficient execution.
     4→
     5→## Overview
     6→
     7→Forge transforms a project specification into executable phases, running Claude AI against each phase with structured prompts. It monitors for completion signals, manages context to prevent overflow, tracks file changes, provides intelligent approval gates, and maintains comprehensive audit logs.
     8→
     9→## Features
    10→
    11→- **Phase-Based Execution**: Break complex projects into sequential phases with iteration budgets
    12→- **Hook System**: Event-driven extensibility with command and prompt hooks
    13→- **Skills/Templates**: Reusable prompt fragments loaded on-demand
    14→- **Context Compaction**: Automatic summarization to prevent context overflow
    15→- **Sub-Phase Delegation**: Spawn child phases for scope discovery
    16→- **Permission Modes**: Varying oversight levels (strict, standard, autonomous, readonly)
    17→- **Pattern Learning**: Capture and reuse patterns from successful projects
    18→- **Progress Signaling**: Intermediate status updates, blockers, and pivots
    19→
    20→## Installation
    21→
    22→### Prerequisites
    23→
    24→- Rust toolchain (Edition 2024)
    25→- Git
    26→- [Claude CLI](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) installed and in PATH
    27→
    28→### Build
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→cargo build --release
    32→```
    33→
    34→### Install
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# Option 1: Install via cargo
    38→cargo install --path .
    39→
    40→# Option 2: Symlink to PATH
    41→ln -s $(pwd)/target/release/forge ~/.local/bin/forge
    42→```
    43→
    44→## Quick Start
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→# Initialize a new project
    48→forge init
    49→
    50→# Create a spec interactively
    51→forge interview
    52→
    53→# Generate phases from the spec
    54→forge generate
    55→
    56→# Start the orchestration loop
    57→forge run
    58→
    59→# View phases
    60→forge list
    61→
    62→# Check progress
    63→forge status
    64→```
    65→
    66→## Commands
    67→
    68→### Core Workflow
    69→
    70→| Command | Description |
    71→|---------|-------------|
    72→| `forge init` | Initialize `.forge/` directory structure |
    73→| `forge interview` | Interactive spec generation |
    74→| `forge generate` | Create phases from spec |
    75→| `forge run` | Execute phases sequentially |
    76→| `forge run --phase 07` | Start from specific phase |
    77→| `forge phase <N>` | Run a single phase |
    78→| `forge list` | Display all phases |
    79→| `forge status` | Show progress |
    80→| `forge reset` | Reset all progress |
    81→| `forge factory` | Launch the Factory Kanban board UI |
    82→
    83→### Configuration
    84→
    85→| Command | Description |
    86→|---------|-------------|
    87→| `forge config show` | Display current configuration |
    88→| `forge config validate` | Check configuration for issues |
    89→| `forge config init` | Create default `forge.toml` |
    90→
    91→### Skills Management
    92→
    93→| Command | Description |
    94→|---------|-------------|
    95→| `forge skills` | List all available skills |
    96→| `forge skills show <name>` | Display skill content |
    97→| `forge skills create <name>` | Create a new skill |
    98→| `forge skills delete <name>` | Delete a skill |
    99→
   100→### Pattern Learning
   101→
   102→| Command | Description |
   103→|---------|-------------|
   104→| `forge learn` | Learn pattern from current project |
   105→| `forge patterns` | List learned patterns |
   106→| `forge patterns show <name>` | Show pattern details |
   107→| `forge patterns stats` | Aggregate statistics |
   108→| `forge patterns recommend` | Suggest patterns for spec |
   109→| `forge patterns compare` | Compare two patterns |
   110→
   111→### Context Management
   112→
   113→| Command | Description |
   114→|---------|-------------|
   115→| `forge compact` | Manually trigger compaction |
   116→| `forge compact --status` | Show compaction status |
   117→
   118→### Audit
   119→
   120→| Command | Description |
   121→|---------|-------------|
   122→| `forge audit show <phase>` | View phase audit |
   123→| `forge audit changes` | Show file changes |
   124→| `forge audit export <file>` | Export audit to JSON |
   125→
   126→## Global Options
   127→
   128→| Option | Description |
   129→|--------|-------------|
   130→| `-v, --verbose` | Enable verbose output |
   131→| `--yes` | Auto-approve all phases |
   132→| `--auto-approve-threshold <N>` | Auto-approve when file changes ≤ N (default: 5) |
   133→| `--project-dir <PATH>` | Project directory |
   134→| `--spec-file <PATH>` | Path to spec file |
   135→| `--context-limit <LIMIT>` | Context limit (e.g., "80%" or "500000") |
   136→
   137→## Configuration
   138→
   139→### forge.toml
   140→
   141→Create `.forge/forge.toml` for project configuration:
   142→
   143→```toml
   144→[project]
   145→name = "my-project"
   146→claude_cmd = "claude"
   147→
   148→[defaults]
   149→budget = 8
   150→auto_approve_threshold = 5
   151→permission_mode = "standard"  # strict, standard, autonomous, readonly
   152→context_limit = "80%"
   153→skip_permissions = true
   154→
   155→# Phase-specific overrides using glob patterns
   156→[phases.overrides."database-*"]
   157→permission_mode = "strict"
   158→budget = 12
   159→
   160→[phases.overrides."*-readonly"]
   161→permission_mode = "readonly"
   162→
   163→# Global skills (loaded for all phases)
   164→[skills]
   165→global = ["rust-conventions"]
   166→
   167→# Hook definitions
   168→[[hooks.definitions]]
   169→event = "PrePhase"
   170→match_pattern = "*database*"
   171→command = "./scripts/ensure-db.sh"
   172→
   173→[[hooks.definitions]]
   174→event = "OnApproval"
   175→type = "prompt"
   176→prompt = "Should we proceed? Return {approve: bool, reason: str}"
   177→```
   178→
   179→### Environment Variables
   180→
   181→| Variable | Description | Default |
   182→|----------|-------------|---------|
   183→| `CLAUDE_CMD` | Claude CLI command | `claude` |
   184→| `SKIP_PERMISSIONS` | Skip permission prompts | `true` |
   185→| `FORGE_CMD` | Forge CLI command for pipeline execution | `forge` |
   186→
   187→## Hook System
   188→
   189→Hooks intercept lifecycle events for custom behavior:
   190→
   191→### Hook Events
   192→
   193→| Event | Description |
   194→|-------|-------------|
   195→| `PrePhase` | Before phase execution |
   196→| `PostPhase` | After phase completion |
   197→| `PreIteration` | Before each Claude invocation |
   198→| `PostIteration` | After each Claude response |
   199→| `OnFailure` | When phase exceeds budget |
   200→| `OnApproval` | When approval gate is presented |
   201→
   202→### Hook Types
   203→
   204→**Command Hooks**: Execute bash scripts with JSON input
   205→
   206→```toml
   207→[[hooks.definitions]]
   208→event = "PrePhase"
   209→command = "./scripts/setup.sh"
   210→```
   211→
   212→**Prompt Hooks**: Use LLM to evaluate conditions
   213→
   214→```toml
   215→[[hooks.definitions]]
   216→event = "PostIteration"
   217→type = "prompt"
   218→prompt = "Did Claude make meaningful progress? Return {continue: bool}"
   219→```
   220→
   221→## Orchestrator Lifecycle
   222→
   223→Each `forge run` invocation moves through a well-defined lifecycle for every phase.
   224→Understanding this lifecycle is essential when writing hooks, debugging failures, or reading logs.
   225→
   226→### Phase Lifecycle
   227→
   228→```
   229→forge run
   230→  └─ For each phase (sequential) or wave of phases (DAG/swarm):
   231→       1. ApprovalGate.check_phase()          ← GateDecision: Approved | Rejected | Aborted
   232→       2. Hook: PrePhase                       ← runs before any iteration starts
   233→       3. For each iteration (1 → budget):
   234→            a. Hook: PreIteration
   235→            b. Claude invocation (streaming)
   236→            c. Parse output for:
   237→               - <promise>…</promise>          ← phase completion signal
   238→               - <progress>N%</progress>       ← updates UI progress bar
   239→               - <blocker>…</blocker>          ← displayed to operator
   240→               - <pivot>…</pivot>              ← injected into next iteration prompt
   241→               - <spawn_subphase>{…}           ← triggers sub-phase
   242→            d. Collect git diff (file changes)
   243→            e. Hook: PostIteration
   244→            f. If promise found → break (phase complete)
   245→            g. If budget exhausted → Hook: OnFailure → phase fails
   246→       4. Hook: PostPhase
   247→       5. (If swarm) Hook: OnApproval for review specialists
   248→```
   249→
   250→### Hook Invocation Order
   251→
   252→| Event | When | Typical use |
   253→|-------|------|-------------|
   254→| `PrePhase` | After gate approval, before iteration 1 | Set up databases, fetch secrets |
   255→| `PostPhase` | After all iterations complete | Run test suite, notify Slack |
   256→| `PreIteration` | Before each Claude invocation | Inject context, update prompts |
   257→| `PostIteration` | After each Claude response is parsed | Log token usage, trigger CI |
   258→| `OnFailure` | When budget is exhausted without a promise | Alert on-call, open incident |
   259→| `OnApproval` | When the approval gate presents a prompt | LLM-assisted decision making |
   260→
   261→### Permission Mode Lifecycle Differences
   262→
   263→| Mode | Phase approval | Iteration approval | File writes |
   264→|------|---------------|-------------------|-------------|
   265→| `strict` | Interactive prompt | Interactive prompt | Allowed |
   266→| `standard` | Auto if ≤ threshold file changes | Auto-continue | Allowed |
   267→| `autonomous` | Auto-approve | Auto-continue; prompt if 3+ stale iters | Allowed |
   268→| `readonly` | Auto-approve | Auto-continue | **Blocked** |
   269→
   270→### Promise Detection
   271→
   272→Claude signals phase completion by emitting:
   273→
   274→```xml
   275→<promise>DONE</promise>
   276→```
   277→
   278→The token inside the tags must match the phase's configured `promise` string (case-sensitive). Once detected, the orchestrator stops iterations and advances to the next phase.
   279→
   280→## Skills System
   281→
   282→Skills are reusable prompt fragments in `.forge/skills/`:
   283→
   284→```
   285→.forge/skills/
   286→├── rust-conventions/
   287→│   └── SKILL.md
   288→├── testing-strategy/
   289→│   └── SKILL.md
   290→└── api-design/
   291→    └── SKILL.md
   292→```
   293→
   294→Reference skills in phases:
   295→
   296→```json
   297→{
   298→  "number": "03",
   299→  "name": "API implementation",
   300→  "skills": ["rust-conventions", "api-design"],
   301→  "promise": "API COMPLETE"
   302→}
   303→```
   304→
   305→## Permission Modes
   306→
   307→| Mode | Description |
   308→|------|-------------|
   309→| `strict` | Require approval for every iteration |
   310→| `standard` | Approve phase start, auto-continue iterations |
   311→| `autonomous` | Auto-approve if within budget and making progress |
   312→| `readonly` | Research/planning only, no file modifications |
   313→
   314→## Progress Signaling
   315→
   316→Beyond binary promise detection, Claude can output intermediate signals:
   317→
   318→```xml
   319→<progress>50%</progress>     <!-- Partial completion -->
   320→<blocker>Need X</blocker>    <!-- Pause and prompt user -->
   321→<pivot>New approach Y</pivot> <!-- Strategy shift logged -->
   322→```
   323→
   324→## Sub-Phase Delegation
   325→
   326→Phases can spawn child phases for discovered scope:
   327→
   328→```xml
   329→<spawn_subphase>
   330→{
   331→  "name": "Set up OAuth provider",
   332→  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
   333→  "budget": 5,
   334→  "reasoning": "OAuth setup is complex enough for its own phase"
   335→}
   336→</spawn_subphase>
   337→```
   338→
   339→Sub-phases:
   340→- Inherit parent's git snapshot as baseline
   341→- Have independent budgets carved from parent
   342→- Must complete before parent can complete
   343→- Maintain hierarchical audit trail
   344→
   345→## Context Compaction
   346→
   347→Forge tracks context usage and automatically summarizes prior iterations when approaching limits:

   350→- Discards: verbose intermediate outputs, superseded attempts
   351→- Configurable threshold via `--context-limit` or `forge.toml`

   497→## Swarm Orchestration
   498→
   499→Forge supports parallel phase execution through a hybrid swarm architecture that combines native Rust DAG scheduling with Claude Code's agent coordination capabilities.
   500→
   501→### Overview
   502→
   503→The swarm system provides:
   504→
   505→- **Parallel Phase Execution**: Run independent phases simultaneously via DAG scheduling
   506→- **Review Specialists**: Automated code review with security, performance, and architecture analysis
   507→- **Dynamic Decomposition**: Automatically split complex phases into smaller tasks
   508→- **LLM Arbiter**: Autonomous resolution of review failures without human intervention
   509→- **Checkpoint Recovery**: Resume interrupted runs from saved state
   510→
   511→### Swarm Commands
   512→
   513→| Command | Description |
   514→|---------|-------------|
   515→| `forge swarm` | Execute phases in parallel using DAG scheduler |
   516→| `forge swarm --from 05` | Start parallel execution from phase 05 |
   517→| `forge swarm --only 07` | Run only phase 07 |
   518→| `forge swarm status` | Show current swarm execution status |
   519→| `forge swarm abort` | Gracefully stop running swarm |
   520→
   521→### Swarm Options
   522→
   523→| Option | Description | Default |
   524→|--------|-------------|---------|
   525→| `--max-parallel <N>` | Maximum concurrent phases | 4 |
   526→| `--backend <TYPE>` | Execution backend: auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2 | auto |
   527→| `--review <SPECIALISTS>` | Enable review: security, performance, architecture, simplicity, all | none |
   528→| `--review-mode <MODE>` | Resolution mode: manual, auto, arbiter | manual |
   529→| `--max-fix-attempts <N>` | Maximum auto-fix attempts | 2 |
   530→| `--escalate-on <TYPES>` | Always escalate these finding types (comma-separated) | none |
   531→| `--arbiter-confidence <N>` | Minimum confidence for arbiter (0.0-1.0) | 0.7 |
   532→| `--decompose` | Enable dynamic decomposition | true |
   533→| `--no-decompose` | Disable dynamic decomposition | |
   534→| `--decompose-threshold <N>` | Budget percentage to trigger decomposition | 50 |
   535→| `--permission-mode <MODE>` | Permission mode: strict, standard, autonomous | standard |
   536→| `--ui <MODE>` | Output format: full, minimal, json | full |
   537→| `--fail-fast` | Stop all phases on first failure | disabled |
   538→
   539→### How DAG Scheduling Works
   540→
   541→Forge analyzes phase dependencies and computes execution waves:
   542→
   543→```
   544→phases.json with dependencies:
   545→  01 (no deps) ─┐
   546→  02 → [01]     │ Wave 1: [01]
   547→  03 → [01]     ├ Wave 2: [02, 03, 04]
   548→  04 → [01]     │ Wave 3: [05, 06]
   549→  05 → [02, 03] │ Wave 4: [07]
   550→  06 → [03, 04] │
   551→  07 → [05, 06] ┘
   552→
   553→Phases in the same wave run in parallel (up to --max-parallel).
   554→```
   555→
   556→### Phase Dependencies
   557→
   558→Define dependencies in `phases.json`:
   559→
   560→```json
   561→{
   562→  "number": "05",
   563→  "name": "OAuth integration",
   564→  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
   565→  "budget": 10,
   566→  "depends_on": ["02", "03"]
   567→}
   568→```
   569→
   570→### Review Specialists
   571→
   572→Review specialists automatically analyze completed phases:
   573→
   574→| Specialist | Focus Areas | Gate |
   575→|------------|-------------|------|
   576→| `security` | Injection risks, auth issues, secrets exposure | Yes |
   577→| `performance` | N+1 queries, memory leaks, algorithmic complexity | No |
   578→| `architecture` | SOLID violations, coupling, separation of concerns | Yes |
   579→| `simplicity` | Over-engineering, premature abstraction, YAGNI | No |
   580→
   581→**Gating reviews** block phase completion until issues are resolved.
   582→**Advisory reviews** report findings but don't block progress.
   583→
   584→Enable reviews:
   585→
   586→```bash
   587→# Single specialist
   588→forge swarm --review security
   589→
   590→# Multiple specialists
   591→forge swarm --review security,performance
   592→
   593→# All specialists
   594→forge swarm --review all
   595→```
   596→
   597→### Review Output
   598→
   599→```json
   600→{
   601→  "phase": "05",
   602→  "reviewer": "security-sentinel",
   603→  "verdict": "warn",
   604→  "findings": [
   605→    {
   606→      "severity": "warning",
   607→      "file": "src/auth/oauth.rs",
   608→      "line": 142,
   609→      "issue": "Token stored in localStorage is vulnerable to XSS",
   610→      "suggestion": "Use httpOnly cookies instead"
   611→    }
   612→  ]
   613→}
   614→```
   615→
   616→### Resolution Modes
   617→
   618→When a gating review fails, three resolution modes are available:
   619→
   620→| Mode | Behavior |
   621→|------|----------|
   622→| `manual` | Always pause for user input |
   623→| `auto` | Attempt auto-fix, retry up to 2 times |
   624→| `arbiter` | LLM decides based on severity and context |
   625→
   626→The **arbiter** mode uses an LLM to analyze findings and decide:
   627→
   628→- **PROCEED**: Continue despite findings (style issues, false positives)
   629→- **FIX**: Spawn fix agent and retry (clear fix path exists)
   630→- **ESCALATE**: Require human decision (architectural concerns)
   631→
   632→```bash
   633→# Use arbiter with 80% confidence threshold
   634→forge swarm --review security --review-mode arbiter --arbiter-confidence 0.8
   635→```
   636→
   637→### Dynamic Decomposition
   638→
   639→When a phase is too complex, Forge can automatically decompose it:
   640→
   641→**Triggers:**
   642→- Worker emits `<blocker>` with complexity signal
   643→- Iterations exceed 50% budget with progress < 30%
   644→- Worker requests: `<request-decomposition/>`
   645→
   646→**Example decomposition:**
   647→
   648→```
   649→Phase 05: OAuth Integration (budget: 20)
   650→         │
   651→         │ Detected: "3 separate provider integrations needed"
   652→         ▼
   653→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   654→│ Decomposition produces:                │
   655→│ ├── 05.1: Google OAuth (budget: 5) ─┐  │
   656→│ ├── 05.2: GitHub OAuth (budget: 5) ─┼─ parallel
   657→│ ├── 05.3: Auth0 OAuth  (budget: 5) ─┘  │
   658→│ └── 05.4: Unified handler (budget: 3)  │
   659→│           depends_on: [05.1-3]         │
   660→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   661→```
   662→
   663→### Swarm Configuration
   664→
   665→Configure swarm behavior in `forge.toml`:
   666→
   667→```toml
   668→[swarm]
   669→enabled = true
   670→backend = "auto"                    # auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2
   671→default_strategy = "adaptive"
   672→max_agents = 5
   673→
   674→[swarm.reviews]
   675→enabled = true
   676→specialists = ["security", "performance"]
   677→mode = "arbiter"                    # manual, auto, arbiter
   678→
   679→# Per-phase swarm overrides
   680→[phases.overrides."*-complex"]
   681→swarm = { strategy = "parallel", max_agents = 4 }
   682→
   683→[phases.overrides."*-refactor"]
   684→swarm = { strategy = "wave_pipeline", reviews = ["architecture"] }
   685→```

   689→Enable swarm execution for specific phases:
   690→
   691→```json

   693→  "number": "05",
   694→  "name": "OAuth integration",
   695→  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
   696→  "budget": 15,
   697→  "swarm": {
   698→    "strategy": "parallel",
   699→    "max_agents": 4,
   700→    "reviews": ["security"]
   701→  }
   702→}

   762→### Troubleshooting
   763→
   764→#### Swarm won't start
   765→
   766→```
   767→Error: No phases.json found
   768→```
   769→
   770→Run `forge generate` first to create phases from your spec.
   771→
   772→#### Phase stuck in "Blocked" state
   773→
   774→Check dependencies with `forge list --deps`. A phase stays blocked until all its dependencies complete successfully.
   775→
   776→#### Review keeps failing
   777→
   778→1. Check the review findings in the output
   779→2. Try `--review-mode auto` to attempt automatic fixes
   780→3. Use `--review-mode arbiter` for LLM-assisted resolution
   781→4. Lower the gating threshold or remove the specialist from gating
   782→
   783→#### Out of memory during parallel execution
   784→
   785→Reduce `--max-parallel` to limit concurrent Claude instances:
   786→
   787→```bash
   788→forge swarm --max-parallel 2
   789→```
   790→
   791→#### Checkpoint recovery fails
   792→
   793→Clear checkpoints and restart:
   794→
   795→```bash
   796→rm -rf .forge/checkpoints/
   797→forge swarm
   798→```

   800→## Factory (Kanban Board)
   801→
   802→Forge Factory is a web-based Kanban board for managing issues that can self-implement through the Forge orchestration engine. Launch it with:
   803→
   804→```bash
   805→forge factory
   806→```
   807→
   808→This starts a local web server (default port 3000) with a drag-and-drop Kanban board and real-time pipeline progress.
   809→
   810→### Kanban Columns
   811→
   812→| Column | Description |
   813→|--------|-------------|
   814→| **Backlog** | New issues waiting to be worked on |
   815→| **Ready** | Issues prioritized and ready for pipeline execution |
   816→| **In Progress** | Issues currently being implemented by a pipeline |
   817→| **In Review** | Pipeline completed, PR created and awaiting review |
   818→| **Done** | Issue resolved and merged |
   819→
   820→Issues can be dragged between columns manually, or they move automatically during pipeline execution.
   821→
   822→### Self-Implementing Issues
   823→
   824→Clicking **"Run Pipeline"** on an issue triggers the full implementation lifecycle:
   825→
   826→1. **Column moves to In Progress** — issue is picked up
   827→2. **Git branch created** — `forge/issue-{id}-{slug}` branch for isolation
   828→3. **Phases auto-generated** — issue description written to `.forge/spec.md`, then `forge generate` creates `phases.json`
   829→4. **Pipeline executes** — `forge swarm` runs if phases exist, otherwise falls back to `claude --print` for simple issues
   830→5. **PR created** — on success, a pull request is opened via `gh pr create`
   831→6. **Column moves to In Review** — signals the work is ready for human review
   832→
   833→### Real-Time Progress
   834→
   835→The Factory UI receives live updates via WebSocket:
   836→
   837→| Event | Description |
   838→|-------|-------------|
   839→| `PipelineStarted` | Pipeline execution begins |
   840→| `PipelineBranchCreated` | Git branch created for the issue |
   841→| `PipelinePhaseStarted` | A DAG phase begins execution |
   842→| `PipelinePhaseCompleted` | A DAG phase finishes |
   843→| `PipelineReviewStarted` | Code review begins for a phase |
   844→| `PipelineReviewCompleted` | Code review finishes with findings |
   845→| `PipelinePrCreated` | Pull request created on success |
   846→| `PipelineCompleted` | Pipeline finishes (success or failure) |
   847→
   848→The issue detail panel shows a **Phase Timeline** with per-phase status, iteration progress, and duration.
   849→
   850→### Factory Options
   851→
   852→| Option | Description | Default |
   853→|--------|-------------|---------|
   854→| `--port <PORT>` | HTTP server port | `3000` |
   855→| `--db-path <PATH>` | SQLite database file | `.forge/factory.db` |
   856→
   857→### Factory REST API
   858→
   859→| Method | Endpoint | Description |
   860→|--------|----------|-------------|
   861→| `GET` | `/api/board` | Get board view with all columns and issues |
   862→| `POST` | `/api/issues` | Create a new issue |
   863→| `GET` | `/api/issues/:id` | Get issue detail with pipeline runs and phases |
   864→| `PUT` | `/api/issues/:id` | Update an issue |
   865→| `DELETE` | `/api/issues/:id` | Delete an issue |
   866→| `PUT` | `/api/issues/:id/move` | Move issue to a different column |
   867→| `POST` | `/api/issues/:id/pipeline` | Trigger pipeline execution |
   868→| `POST` | `/api/pipeline-runs/:id/cancel` | Cancel a running pipeline |
   869→| `GET` | `/ws` | WebSocket endpoint for real-time updates |

     9→
    10→## Executive Summary
    11→
    12→This document describes the design for integrating parallel execution and swarm orchestration into Forge. The chosen approach is a **hybrid architecture**:
    13→
    14→- **Native Rust DAG scheduler** for phase-level parallelism (fast, predictable, testable)
    15→- **Swarm hooks** that delegate to Claude Code's TeammateTool for within-phase complexity
    16→
    17→This gives us the best of both worlds: disciplined orchestration where Forge excels, and sophisticated agent coordination where Claude Code excels.

    35→
    36→## Goals & Non-Goals
    37→
    38→### Goals
    39→
    40→1. **Faster execution** - Run independent phases in parallel via native DAG scheduling
    41→2. **Better quality** - Swarm-based parallel review specialists gate phase completion
    42→3. **Smarter decomposition** - Automatically split complex phases using Claude agents
    43→4. **Autonomous operation** - LLM arbiter can resolve review failures without human intervention
    44→5. **Resilience** - Recover gracefully from crashes via checkpointing
    45→6. **Predictable behavior** - Native scheduling eliminates LLM latency variance in critical path
    46→7. **Backwards compatibility** - Existing `forge run` continues to work
    47→
    48→### Non-Goals
    49→
    50→- Replacing Claude Code's swarm primitives with native implementations
    51→- Supporting non-Claude LLM backends (initially)
    52→- Real-time collaboration between human and swarm
    53→- Distributed execution across multiple machines
    54→

    56→
    57→## Architecture Overview
    58→
    59→The hybrid architecture splits responsibilities clearly:
    60→
    61→```
    62→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    63→│                            FORGE (Native Rust)                           │
    64→│                                                                          │
    65→│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
    66→│  │                        DAG SCHEDULER                                │ │
    67→│  │                                                                     │ │
    68→│  │   phases.json ──▶ [Build Graph] ──▶ [Compute Waves] ──▶ [Dispatch] │ │
    69→│  │                                                                     │ │
    70→│  │   Wave 1: [01]──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ │
    71→│  │   Wave 2: [02]─────┬─────[03]─────┬─────[06]────────────────────┤  │ │
    72→│  │   Wave 3: [04]─────┴─────[05*]────┴─────[07]────────────────────┤  │ │

   116→## Native DAG Scheduler
   117→
   118→### Core Data Structures
   119→
   120→```rust
   121→// src/dag/mod.rs
   122→
   123→use petgraph::graph::{DiGraph, NodeIndex};
   124→use petgraph::algo::toposort;
   125→use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
   126→use tokio::sync::mpsc;
   127→
   128→/// The DAG scheduler manages parallel phase execution
   129→pub struct DagScheduler {
   130→    /// Directed graph of phase dependencies
   131→    graph: DiGraph<PhaseNode, ()>,
   132→    /// Map from phase number to graph node index
   133→    node_map: HashMap<String, NodeIndex>,
   134→    /// Current execution state
   135→    state: DagState,
   136→    /// Configuration
   137→    config: DagConfig,
   138→}
   139→
   140→/// A node in the phase DAG
   141→pub struct PhaseNode {
   142→    pub phase: Phase,
   143→    pub status: PhaseStatus,
   144→    pub result: Option<PhaseResult>,
   145→}
   146→
   147→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
   148→pub enum PhaseStatus {
   149→    Pending,
   150→    Blocked { waiting_on: Vec<String> },
   151→    Ready,
   152→    Running { started_at: Instant },
   153→    Completed { iterations: u32 },
   154→    Failed { error: String },
   155→    Skipped,
   156→}
   157→
   158→pub struct DagConfig {
   159→    /// Maximum phases to run in parallel
   160→    pub max_parallel: usize,
   161→    /// Stop all phases on first failure
   162→    pub fail_fast: bool,
   163→    /// Enable swarm hooks for marked phases
   164→    pub swarm_enabled: bool,
   165→    /// Backend for swarm execution
   166→    pub swarm_backend: SwarmBackend,
   167→}
   168→
   169→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   170→pub enum SwarmBackend {
   171→    Auto,
   172→    InProcess,
   173→    Tmux,
   174→    Iterm2,
   175→}
   176→```
   177→
   178→### DAG Construction
   179→
   180→```rust
   181→// src/dag/builder.rs
   182→
   183→impl DagScheduler {
   184→    /// Build DAG from phases.json
   185→    pub fn from_phases(phases: &[Phase], config: DagConfig) -> Result<Self> {
   186→        let mut graph = DiGraph::new();
   187→        let mut node_map = HashMap::new();
   188→
   189→        // Add all phases as nodes
   190→        for phase in phases {
   191→            let node = PhaseNode {
   192→                phase: phase.clone(),
   193→                status: PhaseStatus::Pending,
   194→                result: None,
   195→            };
   196→            let idx = graph.add_node(node);
   197→            node_map.insert(phase.number.clone(), idx);
   198→        }
   199→
   200→        // Add dependency edges
   201→        for phase in phases {
   202→            let to_idx = node_map[&phase.number];
   203→            for dep in &phase.depends_on {
   204→                let from_idx = node_map.get(dep)
   205→                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Unknown dependency: {}", dep))?;
   206→                graph.add_edge(*from_idx, to_idx, ());
   207→            }
   208→        }
   209→
   210→        // Verify no cycles
   211→        toposort(&graph, None)
   212→            .map_err(|_| anyhow!("Cycle detected in phase dependencies"))?;
   213→
   214→        Ok(Self {
   215→            graph,
   216→            node_map,
   217→            state: DagState::Idle,
   218→            config,
   219→        })
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    /// Compute execution waves (phases that can run in parallel)
   223→    pub fn compute_waves(&self) -> Vec<Vec<String>> {
   224→        let mut waves = Vec::new();
   225→        let mut completed: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
   226→
   227→        loop {
   228→            // Find all phases whose dependencies are satisfied
   229→            let ready: Vec<String> = self.graph.node_indices()
   230→                .filter_map(|idx| {
   231→                    let node = &self.graph[idx];
   232→                    let phase_num = &node.phase.number;
   233→
   234→                    if completed.contains(phase_num) {
   235→                        return None;
   236→                    }
   237→
   238→                    let deps_satisfied = node.phase.depends_on.iter()
   239→                        .all(|dep| completed.contains(dep));
   240→
   241→                    if deps_satisfied {
   242→                        Some(phase_num.clone())
   243→                    } else {
   244→                        None
   245→                    }
   246→                })
   247→                .collect();
   248→
   249→            if ready.is_empty() {
   250→                break;
   251→            }
   252→
   253→            completed.extend(ready.iter().cloned());
   254→            waves.push(ready);
   255→        }
   256→
   257→        waves
   258→    }
   259→}
   260→```
   261→
   262→### Parallel Executor
   263→
   264→```rust
   265→// src/dag/executor.rs
   266→
   267→impl DagScheduler {
   268→    /// Execute all phases respecting dependencies and parallelism limits
   269→    pub async fn execute(&mut self, runner: &ClaudeRunner) -> Result<DagResult> {
   270→        self.state = DagState::Running;
   271→
   272→        let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel::<PhaseEvent>(100);
   273→        let semaphore = Arc::new(Semaphore::new(self.config.max_parallel));
   274→
   275→        // Track active tasks
   276→        let mut active_tasks: HashMap<String, JoinHandle<PhaseResult>> = HashMap::new();
   277→
   278→        loop {
   279→            // Find phases ready to run
   280→            let ready = self.get_ready_phases();
   281→
   282→            // Spawn tasks for ready phases (respecting semaphore)
   283→            for phase_num in ready {
   284→                let permit = semaphore.clone().acquire_owned().await?;
   285→                let phase = self.get_phase(&phase_num).clone();
   286→                let runner = runner.clone();
   287→                let tx = tx.clone();
   288→                let swarm_enabled = self.is_swarm_phase(&phase_num);
   289→
   290→                let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
   291→                    let _permit = permit; // Hold until complete
   292→
   293→                    let result = if swarm_enabled {
   294→                        execute_swarm_phase(&phase, &runner).await
   295→                    } else {
   296→                        execute_standard_phase(&phase, &runner).await
   297→                    };
   298→
   299→                    tx.send(PhaseEvent::Completed {
   300→                        phase: phase.number.clone(),
   301→                        result: result.clone(),
   302→                    }).await.ok();
   303→
   304→                    result
   305→                });
   306→
   307→                active_tasks.insert(phase_num.clone(), handle);
   308→                self.mark_running(&phase_num);
   309→            }
   310→
   311→            // Wait for any phase to complete
   312→            if active_tasks.is_empty() && self.all_complete() {
   313→                break;
   314→            }
   315→
   316→            match rx.recv().await {
   317→                Some(PhaseEvent::Completed { phase, result }) => {
   318→                    active_tasks.remove(&phase);
   319→                    self.record_result(&phase, result)?;
   320→
   321→                    if self.config.fail_fast && result.is_err() {
   322→                        self.cancel_all(&mut active_tasks).await;
   323→                        break;
   324→                    }
   325→                }
   326→                Some(PhaseEvent::Progress { phase, percent }) => {
   327→                    self.update_progress(&phase, percent);
   328→                }
   329→                None => break,
   330→            }
   331→        }
   332→
   333→        self.state = DagState::Completed;
   334→        Ok(self.build_result())
   335→    }
   336→
   337→    fn get_ready_phases(&self) -> Vec<String> {
   338→        self.graph.node_indices()
   339→            .filter_map(|idx| {
   340→                let node = &self.graph[idx];
   341→
   342→                if !matches!(node.status, PhaseStatus::Pending) {
   343→                    return None;
   344→                }
   345→
   346→                let deps_done = node.phase.depends_on.iter().all(|dep| {
   347→                    matches!(
   348→                        self.get_status(dep),
   349→                        PhaseStatus::Completed { .. } | PhaseStatus::Skipped
   350→                    )
   351→                });
   352→
   353→                if deps_done {
   354→                    Some(node.phase.number.clone())
   355→                } else {
   356→                    None
   357→                }
   358→            })
   359→            .collect()
   360→    }
   361→}
   362→```

   443→### Swarm Executor
   444→
   445→```rust
   446→// src/swarm/executor.rs
   447→
   448→pub struct SwarmExecutor {
   449→    claude_cmd: String,
   450→    backend: SwarmBackend,
   451→    callback_server: CallbackServer,
   452→}
   453→
   454→impl SwarmExecutor {
   455→    pub async fn execute(&self, context: SwarmContext) -> Result<SwarmResult> {
   456→        // 1. Start callback server for progress updates
   457→        let callback_url = self.callback_server.start().await?;
   458→
   459→        // 2. Generate the orchestration prompt
   460→        let prompt = self.build_orchestration_prompt(&context, &callback_url)?;
   461→
   462→        // 3. Invoke Claude Code with swarm capabilities
   463→        let mut cmd = Command::new(&self.claude_cmd);
   464→        cmd.arg("--print")
   465→           .arg("--dangerously-skip-permissions")
   466→           .arg("--output-format").arg("stream-json")
   467→           .current_dir(&context.working_dir);
   468→
   469→        let mut child = cmd
   470→            .stdin(Stdio::piped())
   471→            .stdout(Stdio::piped())
   472→            .stderr(Stdio::piped())
   473→            .spawn()?;
   474→
   475→        // 4. Send prompt via stdin
   476→        child.stdin.take().unwrap()
   477→            .write_all(prompt.as_bytes()).await?;
   478→
   479→        // 5. Monitor execution via callbacks and stdout
   480→        let result = self.monitor_execution(child, &context).await?;
   481→
   482→        // 6. Cleanup
   483→        self.callback_server.stop().await?;
   484→
   485→        Ok(result)
   486→    }
   487→
   488→    fn build_orchestration_prompt(
   489→        &self,
   490→        context: &SwarmContext,
   491→        callback_url: &str,
   492→    ) -> Result<String> {
   493→        Ok(format!(r#"
   494→# Forge Swarm Orchestrator
   495→
   496→You are coordinating a swarm for Forge phase {phase_num}: {phase_name}
   497→
   498→## Context
   499→{context_json}
   500→
   501→## Your Responsibilities
   502→
   503→1. **Create team**: `Teammate({{ operation: "spawnTeam", team_name: "forge-{phase_num}" }})`
   504→
   505→2. **Spawn workers** for each task:
   506→   ```javascript
   507→   Task({{
   508→     team_name: "forge-{phase_num}",
   509→     name: "task-{{id}}",
   510→     subagent_type: "general-purpose",
   511→     prompt: "Execute task: {{description}}",
   512→     run_in_background: true
   513→   }})
   514→   ```
   515→
   516→3. **Monitor completion** via inbox messages
   517→
   518→4. **Report progress** to Forge:
   519→   ```bash
   520→   curl -X POST {callback_url}/progress -d '{{"task": "...", "status": "..."}}'
   521→   ```
   522→
   523→5. **Run reviews** if configured (spawn review specialists in parallel)
   524→
   525→6. **Shutdown cleanly** when all tasks complete
   526→
   527→## Completion Signal
   528→
   529→When finished, output:
   530→```xml
   531→<swarm_complete>
   532→{{"success": true, "tasks_completed": [...], "reviews": [...]}}
   533→</swarm_complete>
   534→```
   535→
   536→Begin now.
   537→"#,
   538→            phase_num = context.phase.number,
   539→            phase_name = context.phase.name,
   540→            context_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&context)?,
   541→            callback_url = callback_url,
   542→        ))
   543→    }
   544→}
   545→```

   743→
   744→### Arbiter Prompt
   745→
   746→```markdown
   747→# Review Arbiter
   748→
   749→You are deciding how to handle review findings that would block progress.
   750→
   751→## Context
   752→- Phase: {phase_id} - {phase_name}
   753→- Budget used: {iterations_used}/{budget}
   754→
   755→## Blocking Findings
   756→```json
   757→{findings_json}
   758→```
   759→
   760→## Decision Criteria
   761→
   762→**PROCEED** when:
   763→- Style issues only
   764→- Minor warnings
   765→- False positives
   766→- Acceptable trade-offs for MVP
   767→
   768→**FIX** when:
   769→- Clear fix path exists
   770→- Security/correctness issues
   771→- Within remaining budget
   772→
   773→**ESCALATE** when:
   774→- Architectural concerns
   775→- Ambiguous risk
   776→- Out of budget
   777→- Policy decisions needed
   778→
   779→## Output
   780→```json
   781→{
   782→  "decision": "PROCEED|FIX|ESCALATE",
   783→  "reasoning": "...",
   784→  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
   785→  "fix_instructions": "if FIX",
   786→  "escalation_summary": "if ESCALATE"
   787→}
   788→```
   789→```
   790→
   791→---
   792→
   793→## Dynamic Decomposition
   794→
   795→### Trigger Conditions
   796→
   797→- Worker emits `<blocker>` with complexity signal
   798→- Iterations > 50% budget with progress < 30%
   799→- Worker explicitly requests: `<request-decomposition/>`
   800→
   801→### Decomposition Agent
   802→
   803→The swarm leader spawns a decomposition agent when triggered:
   804→
   805→```rust
   806→// src/swarm/decomposition.rs
   807→
   808→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   809→pub struct DecompositionResult {
   810→    pub analysis: String,
   811→    pub sub_phases: Vec<SubPhaseSpec>,
   812→    pub integration_phase: Option<SubPhaseSpec>,
   813→}
   814→
   815→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   816→pub struct SubPhaseSpec {
   817→    pub number: String,
   818→    pub name: String,
   819→    pub promise: String,
   820→    pub budget: u32,
   821→    pub depends_on: Vec<String>,
   822→    pub can_parallel: bool,
   823→    pub reasoning: String,
   824→}
   825→```
   826→
   827→### Example Decomposition
   828→
   829→```
   830→Phase 05: OAuth Integration (budget: 20)
   831→         │
   832→         │ Worker: "This requires 3 separate provider integrations"
   833→         ▼
   834→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   835→│ Decomposition Agent analyzes...        │
   836→│                                        │
   837→│ Output:                                │
   838→│ ├── 05.1: Google OAuth (budget: 5) ─┐  │
   839→│ ├── 05.2: GitHub OAuth (budget: 5) ─┼─ parallel
   840→│ ├── 05.3: Auth0 OAuth  (budget: 5) ─┘  │
   841→│ └── 05.4: Unified handler (budget: 3)  │
   842→│           depends_on: [05.1-3]         │
   843→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   844→```
   845→
   846→---
   847→
   848→## CLI Interface
   849→
   850→### Commands
   851→
   852→```bash
   853→# Existing (unchanged)
   854→forge run                         # Sequential execution
   855→forge run --phase 05              # Single phase
   856→
   857→# New parallel/swarm commands
   858→forge swarm                       # Parallel DAG execution
   859→forge swarm --from 05             # Start from phase
   860→forge swarm --max-parallel 3      # Limit concurrency
   861→forge swarm --review security,performance
   862→forge swarm --review-mode arbiter --yes
   863→
   864→# Monitoring
   865→forge swarm status                # Show running swarm
   866→forge swarm abort                 # Graceful stop
   867→
   868→# Backwards compatibility
   869→forge run --parallel              # Alias for 'forge swarm'
   870→```
   871→
   872→### Full Flag Reference
   873→
   874→```
   875→forge swarm [OPTIONS]
   876→
   877→EXECUTION:
   878→    --from <PHASE>              Start from specific phase
   879→    --only <PHASES>             Run only these phases (comma-separated)
   880→    --max-parallel <N>          Max concurrent phases [default: 4]
   881→    --backend <TYPE>            auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2
   882→
   883→REVIEWS:
   884→    --review <SPECIALISTS>      security, performance, architecture, all
   885→    --review-mode <MODE>        manual, auto, arbiter [default: manual]
   886→    --max-fix-attempts <N>      Auto-fix attempts [default: 2]
   887→    --escalate-on <TYPES>       Always escalate these findings
   888→    --arbiter-confidence <N>    Min confidence [default: 0.7]
   889→
   890→DECOMPOSITION:
   891→    --decompose                 Enable decomposition [default]
   892→    --no-decompose              Disable decomposition
   893→    --decompose-threshold <N>   Budget % trigger [default: 50]
   894→
   895→APPROVAL:
   896→    --yes                       Auto-approve all prompts
   897→    --permission-mode <MODE>    strict, standard, autonomous
   898→
   899→MONITORING:
   900→    --ui <MODE>                 full, minimal, json [default: full]
   901→```
   902→
   903→### Example Session
   904→
   905→```bash
   906→$ forge swarm --review security --max-parallel 3
   907→
   908→Analyzing phase dependencies...
   909→  22 phases, 8 execution waves
   910→
   911→Wave 1: [01] ████████████ 100%  (3 iterations)
   912→
   913→Wave 2: [02] ████████░░░░  67%
   914→        [03] ████████████ 100%
   915→        [06] ████████████ 100%
   916→
   917→Reviews for [03]:
   918→  ✓ security-sentinel: PASS
   919→
   920→Wave 2: [02] ████████████ 100%  (10 iterations)
   921→
   922→Reviews for [02]:
   923→  ⚠ security-sentinel: WARN (1 finding)
   924→    └─ src/db/queries.rs:42 - Consider parameterized query
   925→
   926→Continuing (non-gating)...
   927→
   928→Wave 3: [04] ████░░░░░░░░  33%
   929→        [05*] Starting swarm...
   930→              └─ Spawning 3 agents for OAuth integration
   931→        [07] ████████████ 100%
   932→```
   933→
   934→---
   935→
   936→## Error Handling & Recovery
   937→
   938→### Failure Taxonomy
   939→
   940→| Category | Failure | Detection | Recovery |
   941→|----------|---------|-----------|----------|
   942→| Phase | Budget exhausted | Native | Mark failed, continue DAG |
   943→| Phase | Promise not found | Native | Retry with hints |
   944→| Swarm | Agent crash | Callback timeout | Respawn from checkpoint |
   945→| Swarm | Leader crash | Process monitor | Resume with state |
   946→| Review | Timeout | Native | Retry once, then skip |
   947→| Review | Gate failure | Native | Apply resolution mode |
   948→| Infra | Claude API error | HTTP status | Exponential backoff |
   949→
   950→### Checkpoint System
   951→
   952→```rust
   953→// src/checkpoint/mod.rs
   954→
   955→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   956→pub struct Checkpoint {
   957→    pub phase: String,
   958→    pub iteration: u32,
   959→    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
   960→    pub progress_percent: u32,
   961→    pub files_changed: Vec<String>,
   962→    pub git_ref: String,
   963→    pub context_summary: String,
   964→}
   965→
   966→impl Checkpoint {
   967→    pub fn save(&self, checkpoint_dir: &Path) -> Result<()> {
   968→        let path = checkpoint_dir.join(format!("{}.json", self.phase));
   969→        let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(self)?;
   970→        fs::write(&path, content)?;
   971→        Ok(())
   972→    }
   973→
   974→    pub fn load(checkpoint_dir: &Path, phase: &str) -> Result<Option<Self>> {
   975→        let path = checkpoint_dir.join(format!("{}.json", phase));
   976→        if !path.exists() {
   977→            return Ok(None);
   978→        }
   979→        let content = fs::read_to_string(&path)?;
   980→        Ok(Some(serde_json::from_str(&content)?))
   981→    }
   982→}
   983→```
   984→
   985→### Recovery Flow
   986→
   987→```bash
   988→# Automatic resume detection
   989→$ forge swarm
   990→Detected incomplete run: forge-run-20260126-150322
   991→  Progress: 14/22 phases
   992→  Checkpoints: 3 phases resumable
   993→
   994→Resume? [Y/n] y
   995→
   996→Recovering...
   997→  ✓ Loaded checkpoints
   998→  ✓ Reconciled state
   999→  ✓ Respawning 3 phases
  1000→
  1001→Continuing from Wave 4...
  1002→```
  1003→
  1004→---
  1005→
  1006→## Implementation Plan
  1007→
  1008→### Module Structure
  1009→
  1010→```
  1011→src/
  1012→├── main.rs                      # Add swarm subcommand
  1013→├── lib.rs                       # Export new modules
  1014→│
  1015→├── dag/                         # NEW: DAG scheduler
  1016→│   ├── mod.rs
  1017→│   ├── builder.rs               # Build graph from phases
  1018→│   ├── scheduler.rs             # Core scheduling logic
  1019→│   ├── executor.rs              # Parallel execution
  1020→│   └── state.rs                 # Execution state tracking
  1021→│
  1022→├── swarm/                       # NEW: Swarm integration
  1023→│   ├── mod.rs
  1024→│   ├── executor.rs              # Swarm hook executor
  1025→│   ├── context.rs               # SwarmContext types
  1026→│   ├── callback.rs              # HTTP callback server
  1027→│   └── prompts.rs               # Orchestration prompts
  1028→│
  1029→├── review/                      # NEW: Review system
  1030→│   ├── mod.rs
  1031→│   ├── specialists.rs           # Specialist definitions
  1032→│   ├── arbiter.rs               # LLM decision maker
  1033→│   └── findings.rs              # Finding types
  1034→│
  1035→├── checkpoint/                  # NEW: Checkpointing
  1036→│   ├── mod.rs
  1037→│   ├── writer.rs
  1038→│   └── recovery.rs
  1039→│
  1040→├── hooks/
  1041→│   ├── types.rs                 # MODIFY: Add SwarmDispatch event
  1042→│   └── executor.rs              # MODIFY: Add swarm execution
  1043→│
  1044→├── phase.rs                     # MODIFY: Add swarm config
  1045→└── forge_config.rs              # MODIFY: Add swarm section
  1046→```
  1047→
  1048→### Dependencies
  1049→
  1050→```toml
  1051→# Cargo.toml additions
  1052→
  1053→[dependencies]
  1054→petgraph = "0.6"          # DAG operations
  1055→axum = "0.7"              # Callback HTTP server
  1056→notify = "6.0"            # File watching
  1057→tokio-stream = "0.1"      # Async streaming
  1058→```
  1059→
  1060→### Timeline
  1061→
  1062→| Phase | Duration | Deliverables |
  1063→|-------|----------|--------------|
  1064→| 1. DAG Core | Week 1-2 | Graph builder, wave computation, basic executor |
  1065→| 2. Parallel Execution | Week 3-4 | Tokio-based parallel dispatch, state tracking |
  1066→| 3. Swarm Hooks | Week 5-6 | SwarmContext, executor, callback server |
  1067→| 4. Reviews | Week 7-8 | Specialists, findings, arbiter |
  1068→| 5. Checkpoints | Week 9-10 | Persistence, recovery, reconciliation |
  1069→| 6. Polish | Week 11-12 | CLI, UI, documentation, testing |
  1070→
  1071→### Estimated Scope
  1072→
  1073→| Module | LOC |
  1074→|--------|-----|
  1075→| `dag/` | ~800 |
  1076→| `swarm/` | ~700 |
  1077→| `review/` | ~600 |
  1078→| `checkpoint/` | ~400 |
  1079→| Config/CLI | ~300 |
  1080→| Tests | ~1,000 |
  1081→| **Total** | **~3,800** |
  1082→
  1083→---
  1084→
  1085→## Open Questions
  1086→
  1087→1. **Swarm backend selection**: Should we auto-detect or require explicit configuration?
  1088→
  1089→2. **Cross-wave reviews**: Should reviews happen per-phase, per-wave, or configurable?
  1090→
  1091→3. **Budget extension**: Can arbiter extend budgets, or is that always human decision?
  1092→
  1093→4. **Partial wave failure**: If some phases in a wave fail, continue with non-dependent phases?
  1094→
  1095→5. **Swarm task granularity**: How fine-grained should decomposition be? File-level? Function-level?
  1096→
  1097→---
  1098→
  1099→## Summary
  1100→
  1101→The hybrid approach gives us:
  1102→
  1103→- **Fast, predictable scheduling** via native Rust DAG
  1104→- **Sophisticated agent coordination** via Claude Code swarms
  1105→- **Quality gates** via parallel review specialists
  1106→- **Autonomous operation** via LLM arbiter
  1107→- **Resilience** via checkpointing and recovery
  1108→- **Clear separation** of concerns between Forge and Claude Code
  1109→
  1110→This architecture plays to the strengths of both systems while maintaining Forge's philosophy of disciplined, observable orchestration.

1→# Forge
     2→
     3→AI-powered development orchestrator that breaks specs into phases and runs Codex iteratively until completion. Enables disciplined agentic development with parallel execution, review gates, and checkpoint recovery.
     4→
     5→## Quick Reference
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→cargo build --release              # Build
     9→cargo test                         # All tests (--lib for unit, --test integration_tests for integration)
    10→cargo run -- <command>             # Run CLI
    11→```
    12→
    13→## Tech Stack
    14→
    15→Rust (Edition 2024), clap v4, tokio v1, petgraph v0.6, git2 v0.20, axum v0.8, anyhow + thiserror v2
    16→
    17→## Key Concepts
    18→
    19→- **Phases:** Steps with iteration budgets; emit `<promise>DONE</promise>` to signal completion
    20→- **Swarm:** Parallel execution via native DAG scheduler (`forge swarm`) with optional review specialists
    21→- **Permission Modes:** strict | standard | autonomous | readonly
    22→- **Hooks:** 6 events (PrePhase, PostPhase, PreIteration, PostIteration, OnFailure, OnApproval)
    23→- **Signals:** `<progress>`, `<blocker>`, `<pivot>` tags parsed from Codex output
    24→- **Reviews:** security, performance, architecture, simplicity — with arbiter resolution
    25→- **Factory:** Kanban board UI (`forge factory`) with self-implementing issues — triggers pipeline execution, auto-branching, auto-PR, and real-time phase progress via WebSocket
    26→
    27→## Where to Look
    28→
    29→| Need | Location |
    30→|------|----------|
    31→| Commands & swarm usage | `README.md` |
    32→| Architecture & design | `.forge/spec.md` |

    37→| Phase definitions | `src/phase.rs` |
    38→| Configuration | `src/forge_config.rs` |
    39→| State persistence | `src/orchestrator/state.rs` |
    40→| Factory API server | `src/factory/api.rs`, `src/factory/server.rs` |

## Summary: The 4 Embedded Agents in Forge

Based on my thorough search through the Forge codebase, I can identify **4 built-in Review Specialist agents** that are embedded in the system. These are not separate autonomous agents, but rather **specialist roles that Claude Code swarms can assume** when running code reviews during swarm execution.

### The 4 Review Specialist Agents

All defined in `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/review/specialists.rs` and `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/context.rs`:

1. **Security Sentinel** (`security-sentinel`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::SecuritySentinel`
   - Focus Areas: SQL injection, XSS, authentication bypass, secrets exposure, input validation, command injection, path traversal, insecure deserialization
   - Default: Gating (blocks phase completion if issues found)
   - Purpose: Security-focused code review

2. **Performance Oracle** (`performance-oracle`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::PerformanceOracle`
   - Focus Areas: N+1 query patterns, missing indexes, memory leaks, algorithmic complexity, unnecessary allocations, blocking async operations, cache issues, inefficient data structures
   - Default: Non-gating (advisory only)
   - Purpose: Performance and resource usage analysis

3. **Architecture Strategist** (`architecture-strategist`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::ArchitectureStrategist`
   - Focus Areas: SOLID violations, coupling, layering, separation of concerns, circular dependencies, abstraction levels, weak interfaces, god objects
   - Default: Gating (blocks phase completion if issues found)
   - Purpose: Architectural and design pattern review

4. **Simplicity Reviewer** (`simplicity-reviewer`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::SimplicityReviewer`
   - Focus Areas: Over-engineering, premature abstraction, YAGNI violations, unnecessary complexity, dead code, overly clever solutions, excessive indirection, configuration abuse
   - Default: Non-gating (advisory only)
   - Purpose: Simplicity and code clarity analysis

### How They Work

The 4 agents operate within the **swarm orchestration system**:

1. **Orchestration**: When `forge swarm` is executed with review specialists enabled (e.g., `--review security,performance`), a **swarm leader agent** is spawned via Claude Code

2. **Task Distribution**: The leader creates a team (via `TeammateTool`) and spawns **worker agents** for task execution

3. **Review Phase**: After tasks complete, the leader spawns **review specialist agents** for each enabled specialist type (can run in parallel with `parallel: true`)

4. **Completion**: Reviews either gate or advisory based on configuration, and results are aggregated in the `<swarm_complete>` signal

### Key Files

- **Specialist Definitions**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/review/specialists.rs` (197 built-in types, can add custom)
- **Swarm Context Types**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/context.rs` (orchestration configuration)
- **Prompt Generation**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/prompts.rs` (instructions for spawning agents)
- **Execution**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/executor.rs` (manages Claude Code swarm lifecycle)

### CLI Usage

```bash
# Run with specific review specialists
forge swarm --review security,architecture

# Run with all built-in reviewers
forge swarm --review all

# Control gating behavior
forge swarm --review security --review-mode arbiter --arbiter-confidence 0.8
```

These agents are not separate CLI commands or independent processes—they're **specialized Claude Code agents spawned on-demand during swarm execution** to provide quality gates before phases complete.

   - Purpose: Security-focused code review

2. **Performance Oracle** (`performance-oracle`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::PerformanceOracle`
   - Focus Areas: N+1 query patterns, missing indexes, memory leaks, algorithmic complexity, unnecessary allocations, blocking async operations, cache issues, inefficient data structures
   - Default: Non-gating (advisory only)
   - Purpose: Performance and resource usage analysis

3. **Architecture Strategist** (`architecture-strategist`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::ArchitectureStrategist`
   - Focus Areas: SOLID violations, coupling, layering, separation of concerns, circular dependencies, abstraction levels, weak interfaces, god objects
   - Default: Gating (blocks phase completion if issues found)
   - Purpose: Architectural and design pattern review

4. **Simplicity Reviewer** (`simplicity-reviewer`)
   - Type: `SpecialistType::SimplicityReviewer`
   - Focus Areas: Over-engineering, premature abstraction, YAGNI violations, unnecessary complexity, dead code, overly clever solutions, excessive indirection, configuration abuse
   - Default: Non-gating (advisory only)
   - Purpose: Simplicity and code clarity analysis

### How They Work

The 4 agents operate within the **swarm orchestration system**:

1. **Orchestration**: When `forge swarm` is executed with review specialists enabled (e.g., `--review security,performance`), a **swarm leader agent** is spawned via Claude Code

2. **Task Distribution**: The leader creates a team (via `TeammateTool`) and spawns **worker agents** for task execution

3. **Review Phase**: After tasks complete, the leader spawns **review specialist agents** for each enabled specialist type (can run in parallel with `parallel: true`)

4. **Completion**: Reviews either gate or advisory based on configuration, and results are aggregated in the `<swarm_complete>` signal

### Key Files

- **Specialist Definitions**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/review/specialists.rs` (197 built-in types, can add custom)
- **Swarm Context Types**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/context.rs` (orchestration configuration)
- **Prompt Generation**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/prompts.rs` (instructions for spawning agents)
- **Execution**: `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/src/swarm/executor.rs` (manages Claude Code swarm lifecycle)

### CLI Usage

```bash
# Run with specific review specialists
forge swarm --review security,architecture

# Run with all built-in reviewers
forge swarm --review all

# Control gating behavior
forge swarm --review security --review-mode arbiter --arbiter-confidence 0.8
```

These agents are not separate CLI commands or independent processes—they're **specialized Claude Code agents spawned on-demand during swarm execution** to provide quality gates before phases complete.

### Key Architectural Insights

1. **No routing library** — Single component tree with view mode toggles
2. **Centralized state** — useMissionControl hook manages all data
3. **Real-time first** — WebSocket updates propagate immediately
4. **Card-based layout** — Everything is a card (runs, issues, projects)

5. **Modal pattern** — NewIssueModal/ProjectSetup for creation dialogs

### Summary

The Factory UI is a **single-page React dashboard** with:
- **Left sidebar:** Project filtering
- **Center:** Grid/list/analytics of active runs and idle issues
- **Right:** Floating action button
- **Top:** Status bar with system stats and view toggle
- **Bottom:** Collapsible event log

All data flows through **useMissionControl hook** (aggregates from API + WebSocket), state updates trigger re-renders, and WebSocket messages provide real-time push updates. Component hierarchy is flat with no routing—just view mode toggles and modals for dialogs.

### WebSocket Integration

**Path:** `/Users/jdsingh/Projects/AI/forge/ui/src/contexts/WebSocketContext.tsx`

Real-time pub/sub system:
- Connects to `/ws` endpoint
- Auto-reconnect logic (exponential backoff, max 20 attempts)
- Message type: `WsMessage` (union of 30+ message types)
- Usage: `useWsSubscribe(callback)` to listen to messages

**Message types include:**
- Pipeline: PipelineStarted, PipelineProgress, PipelineCompleted, PipelineFailed
- Phases: PipelinePhaseStarted, PipelinePhaseCompleted, PipelineReviewStarted/Completed
- Agent: TeamCreated, AgentTaskStarted/Completed/Failed, AgentThinking/Action/Output/Signal
- Merge: MergeStarted/Completed, MergeConflict
- Verification: VerificationResult
- Issues: IssueCreated/Updated/Moved/Deleted
- Projects: ProjectCreated/Deleted
- Output: PipelineOutputEvent, PipelineFileChanged, PipelineOutput

### Existing Detail Views (Templates)

#### AgentRunCard Detail Tabs (Good Template)
- **Output tab** — Shows pipeline/agent output in rich format
  - Black background terminal style
  - Color-coded events by type
  - Auto-scroll
  - Max height with overflow

- **Phases tab** — Shows phase list with icons, names, iteration counts
  - Minimal styling
  - Status indicators

- **Files tab** — Shows branch, PR, file changes
  - Action indicators (+/~/-)
  - Monospace font for paths

#### Analytics View
- Time range selector (7/30/90 days)
- Multiple sections (summary stats, phase table, recent runs)
- Fetch from custom endpoints (`/api/metrics/*`)
- Error/loading states

---

### Key Architectural Insights

1. **No routing library** — Single component tree with view mode toggles
2. **Centralized state** — useMissionControl hook manages all data
3. **Real-time first** — WebSocket updates propagate immediately
4. **Card-based layout** — Everything is a card (runs, issues, projects)
5. **No detailed drill-down** — AgentRunCard is the only detail view; others are modal-based
6. **Responsive but not mobile** — Fixed sidebar, flex layouts, suitable for desktop

---

### Potential Templates for Agents View

1. **AgentRunCard detail structure** — Use the tabbed pattern for agent task details
2. **EventLog styling** — Source-colored tags + timestamps
3. **ProjectSidebar pattern** — Hierarchical list with badges and status indicators
4. **Analytics layout** — Multiple metric sections, time range selector
5. **Modal pattern** — NewIssueModal/ProjectSetup for creation dialogs

---

### Summary

The Factory UI is a **single-page React dashboard** with:
- **Left sidebar:** Project filtering
- **Center:** Grid/list/analytics of active runs and idle issues
- **Right:** Floating action button
- **Top:** Status bar with system stats and view toggle
- **Bottom:** Collapsible event log

All data flows through **useMissionControl hook** (aggregates from API + WebSocket), state updates trigger re-renders, and WebSocket messages provide real-time push updates. Component hierarchy is flat with no routing—just view mode toggles and modals for dialogs.

All 28 tests pass. Here's a summary of the changes:

### Backend (Rust)

**`src/review/specialists.rs`** — Added two methods to `SpecialistType`:
- `description()` — Returns a detailed description of each agent's role and behavior
- `default_gating()` — Returns whether the agent gates by default (Security Sentinel and Architecture Strategist are gating; Performance Oracle and Simplicity Reviewer are advisory)

**`src/factory/api.rs`** — Added:
- `AgentInfo` response struct with `id`, `name`, `description`, `focus_areas`, `default_gating`
- `GET /api/agents` route and `list_agents` handler that returns all 4 built-in specialists

### Frontend (React)

**`ui/src/types/index.ts`** — Added `'agents'` to `ViewMode` union and `AgentInfo` interface

**`ui/src/api/client.ts`** — Added `listAgents()` API call

**`ui/src/components/StatusBar.tsx`** — Added "agents" toggle button in the view mode bar

**`ui/src/components/Agents.tsx`** — New component showing:
- Header with agent count
- Card grid with each agent featuring:
  - Color-coded left border and icon per agent type
  - Name, agent ID (monospace), and gating/advisory badge
  - Description text
  - Expandable focus areas section (click to toggle)
  - CLI usage hint showing the `forge swarm --review` command

**`ui/src/App.tsx`** — Wired the `Agents` component into the view mode switch
